divergence: A plague wiped out 90% of humanity in 2020
story_angle: eyewitness dispatch from the scene with vivid sensory detail
headline: The Last Gathering at Crystal Hall: A Mourning for Lost Radiance
deck: In the dim twilight of a world steeped in decay, survivors assemble to honor the forgotten joys of yesteryear, amidst the spectral echoes of a plague-ravaged society.
article:
As dusk settled over Crystal Hall, the once-celebrated venue stood as a haunting reminder of gatherings filled with laughter—now a mere shadow of its former self. The air, thick with mildew and the faint traces of bygone fragrances, enveloped the weary souls who ventured onto its crumbling floors. The echoes of joy that once resonated here lay silent, stifled by the weight of despair.

The chandeliers, their brilliance long faded and brass tarnished, hung above a crowd dressed in frayed finery, a diverse assembly where hope and sorrow intertwined like the delicate vines of a withering plant. Each attendee bore witness to the devastation wrought by a relentless plague, which claimed ninety lives out of every hundred. Yet, on this poignant night, they embraced their memories, wearing their worn garments as symbols of resilience rather than mere reminders of loss.

As candles flickered against the advancing darkness, the streets surrounding Crystal Hall seemed to be inhabited by phantoms, their forms drifting in the fog like the very memories they sought to evoke. The echoes of laughter, once vibrant, now resonated with a melancholic undertone, stirring the hearts of those teetering on the brink of despair. “We gather not in merriment, but in solemn reverence,” intoned Lord Alfred Kensington, his voice trembling with the weight of remembrance. “Let us honor the departed and, amidst our sorrow, reignite the flickering flame of humanity.”

Yet, this gathering served as both a tribute and a lament—an elegy for culture, a requiem for humanity, diminished by relentless loss. Crystal Hall, now a mausoleum of memories, stood firm against the encroaching night, a final refuge against oblivion as the shadows of a once-vibrant society intertwined with the specters of the past, haunting this fractured landscape of existence.
author: Foreign Bureau

oped.title: Crystal Hall's Requiem: Good Riddance to Vanity's Plague
oped.author: Mrs. E. Thornfield
oped.body: While the remnants gather to weep over Crystal Hall's faded glow, let us speak plainly: this 'lost radiance' was never ours to mourn. In our 1890 world, foreknowing 2020's scythe that will claim nine in ten souls, the elite's crystal follies merely hastened the rot. Their chandeliers illuminated nothing but class rot and empty pomp. Survival demands we torch such illusions, not preserve them. The plague merely finished what Victorian excess began.

classifieds.text: Gentlemen Seekers of Meaningful Engagement: Are you a skilled artisan or tradesman? Our esteemed guild seeks to rebuild society’s fabric. Join us at the Old Mill by the Thames. Competitive wages offered for craftsmanship that revives our beloved London.

Elegant Residences Available: Grand townhouses and charming cottages in the heart of Green Park await discerning tenants. Ideal for families looking to settle in an era of rebirth. Contact Lady Beatrice at number 14 for a private viewing. Secure your spot in this new age!

Invitation to the Grand Reunion: Join us for a soirée celebrating the resilience of humanity! Enjoy an evening of music, dance, and companionship at the Crystal Ballroom, November 15th. Tickets are limited—secure your invitation today to help forge a bonded community!

Rare Healing Elixirs: Proprietor of the Apothecary on Fleet Street offers a selection of restorative tonics and herbal remedies crafted from nature's bounty. Revitalize your spirit and health! Inquire within for personalized consultations.

panels.caption: Lady Vesper addresses the hall: 'These chandeliers once blazed for the living—now they mourn the plague's harvest.'
The beadle steps forward: 'We banned all new lamps last decade; progress only fattened the dead.'
A gaunt survivor mutters: 'Then why are we here—admiring how well the dark suits our rags?'

joke.setup: At the final assembly in Crystal Hall, the chairman solemnly declared the radiance gone forever.
joke.punchline: The survivors nodded, grateful the plague had spared them the trouble of inventing excuses to stay home.
joke.text: At the final assembly in Crystal Hall, the chairman solemnly declared the radiance gone forever. The survivors nodded, grateful the plague had spared them the trouble of inventing excuses to stay home.

sponsor_ads.headline: Revive Your Spirits with Mournful Melodies!
sponsor_ads.body: Visit the Cracked Casket Conservatory, where our ghostly musicians serenade the spirits of the departed. Perfect for those seeking to lament the radiance of yesteryear while tapping their toes to a dirge.
sponsor_ads.tagline: Melodies may or may not summon the departed.

sponsor_ads.headline: Exclusive! The Last Gathering at Crystal Hall: Lovely Remnants for Sale
sponsor_ads.body: Join us at the mourning for lost radiance, where you can purchase gently used mourning attire and tarnished silverware from the dearly departed. A fine collection of last-minute sentiments!

sponsor_ads.headline: Daily Dose of Regret: Freshly Bottled Nostalgia!
sponsor_ads.body: Now you can bottle your longing for a time when humanity thrived! Our elixir captures the essence of what once was—perfect for sipping during your nightly lamentations.
sponsor_ads.tagline: Warning: Tastes best with a pinch of melancholia.

sponsor_ads.headline: Wit’s End: The Plague-Approved Escape Room Experience!
sponsor_ads.body: Join us at our latest attraction! Solve cryptic puzzles and escape our vintage parlor, filled with relics of the post-plague world. Will you emerge victorious, or simply embrace the darkness?
sponsor_ads.tagline: Guaranteed not to be more depressing than reality.
